name: bijou-i18n Runtime
requirements: Tests ARE the Spec roadmap principle

userStory.asA: builder using Bijou shell, components, or future blocks
userStory.iWant: a dedicated runtime localization package with catalogs, locale direction, and lookup APIs
userStory.soThat: localized shell and app content can be resolved consistently without dragging spreadsheet or storage workflows into the runtime

acceptanceCriteria: Bijou defines a dedicated runtime localization package boundary instead of burying i18n helpers ad hoc inside unrelated packages
The runtime package supports namespaced catalog keys and catalog load/unload
The runtime package is usable entirely in memory and assumes no filesystem, browser storage, database, or remote service
The runtime package exposes locale and direction metadata
The runtime package supports string-first message lookup but is not limited to string-only resources
The runtime package provides a path for message, resource, and data entry kinds
The runtime package can resolve runtime-safe references without becoming a spreadsheet interpreter
The runtime package exposes locale-aware formatting seams for values like dates, times, numbers, and lists
The runtime package is cleanly consumable by @flyingrobots/bijou and @flyingrobots/bijou-tui
backwardCompatibility: This is initially additive. Existing English shell copy and in-memory app models can remain valid while the framework introduces a dedicated runtime i18n seam.

inScope: Catalog keys with namespace and id
Message, resource, and data entry kinds
Formatter hooks and locale-aware formatting seams
outScope: Long-string overflow UI policies such as wrap, stack, truncate, or marquee

testPlan.goldenPath: Write failing tests for catalog registration, message/resource lookup, locale and direction switching, reference resolution, and formatter seams. Then integrate the runtime package into one small shell or docs proving surface without requiring any external storage.
edgeCases: Missing translation falls back to source locale
Unknown key lookup remains explicit instead of silently returning misleading text
Reference chains that are invalid or cyclic fail clearly
Direction changes without changing locale-specific message ids
testPlan.fuzzStress: Catalog lookup and direction-aware key resolution may warrant fuzzing later, but v0 should focus on deterministic runtime behavior and clear failure modes.
failureCases: The runtime package assumes spreadsheets or files are present
The runtime package only supports plain strings and has no path for resource or data entries
Direction metadata is left out and shell/layout layers must invent their own locale-direction seams
Reference support turns the runtime into a complex authoring or spreadsheet execution engine

estimatedComplexity.linesOfCode: ~200-450 lines for initial types, in-memory adapter, formatter seams, and focused tests
estimatedComplexity.timeComplexity: Lookup should remain near O(1) by key within loaded catalogs, with reference resolution bounded by normalized reference depth
